sentences.text: Hey, good morning, Catherine.
I'm so sorry.
Not at all, don't worry about it.
I know we've got… I… trust me, I know we've all got a million things going on.
I'm the last person that this wouldn't happen to, so don't worry about it.
How are you?
I'm fine, how are you?
I'm good, I'm good.
Just, catching up.
I feel like every time you take some PTO, it's like a double-edged sword, right?
Yeah.
when you come back, so… But I did want to… I love the idea of maybe meeting at least monthly.
We can always move it, I mean, every six weeks or whatever seems best, but I feel like for now, maybe monthly.
The big thing on my mind is Cortera, and just making sure they feel comfortable with, I guess, what's happening on the Alfred side.
Not sure if you've heard much from them.
I haven't talked to them specifically about this since the last call we were on together with their team.
But I have talked to Alfred, and what Alfred wants is to absorb all of the unit, all of the market surveys into the Alfred account, so that's kind of, like, the one Source of truth for them.
My concern was that I know Cortera's data team is super into their data sync and just their assets, so my proposal was just we keep Cortera's account active no matter what.
Sounds like Heath was cool about them just kind of running their contract out, at least until Alfred's renewal.
Does that.
Yes.
All sound good to you?
Yeah, I just don't want them to have to re-contract because they…
They worked so hard to, like, make sure we didn't….
I know, right, right, right.

I know there was a big push to make sure that the contract got signed during or before the merger, so I don't want to be disrespectful of that at all, so I think… I'm just gonna mention to Sam, I think, on the next one, that we're gonna keep his instance active.
And I almost wonder, do you think it's best to give them the option to either opt into Alfred's account?
or… Alfred seems more or less like they're willing to find a compromise.
I just don't know if it's gonna feel weird for them to be suddenly in the Alfred account, and just be assigned to their properties.
What do you… what is your read on that?
Do you feel anything about that?
… I mean, I feel like with them being such a new customer, that, like, the least amount of… Resisting, or….
Anything.
Yeah, would probably be ideal, but….
I, you know, I don't know how much that…
Is a sticking point for Alfred, so I don't want to put them in a jeopardizing position either.
Yeah.
I don't… I don't think there's any harm in Cortera, just running business like usual.
Yeah.
And then, it's just the same market surveys also exist in the Alfred account for the Alfred people to use.
Okay.
So I think that's how I'm gonna go forward.
Business as usual for Katera, they really shouldn't feel anything different, it's just Alfred has whatever it is, I think 50 or 60 new market surveys in the account.
Yeah.
And then Alfred's renewal is the end of Q4, so I'm thinking at that point, maybe we talk again.
and we say, hey, Alfred, are you going to absorb the new units?
I guess that's a conversation for, you know, another time.
But I guess at that point, it would be like, hey, now we put in the change of management, perhaps.
At least that's what it sounded like.
Heath was saying in that Slack thread.
So, sounds like a problem we don't have to worry about until at least another quarter.
Okay, great.
Cool.
Okay.
That was the big thing I wanted to talk about, just making sure… and I might be over-rotating also, so I just wanted to make sure that I don't sound crazy talking about this.
I've never heard that before, I like that.
Overwritten.
You know, Heath has me using it now.
He says that a lot, which I think is a nice way of saying, like, hey, chill out.
Sorry if my dog barked.
Not at all.
I think you have the cutest dog in the company, personally.
That's my vote.
But she's a mess.
Well, she's a puppy, right?
Yeah.
Puppies be like that.
Yes.
Last thing, gallery.
I know they had a few things come in while I was out.
the… it seems like most of it's cool, they just have a wacky, situation, I guess because we don't have any outs.
And they're like, oh, we don't need the Arlo anymore, and I guess, obviously, they don't want to bill for it anymore.
The most recent property we created for them was the Crescent.
I was just gonna have… And I'm saying this because I think you might feel it, I guess, in the commission, perhaps.
I was gonna propose to billing that we bill for the difference in the crescent units between the Arlo that's coming out, so this way.
So this way, I guess they don't have to feel the Arlo coming out.
And we still bill for whatever the difference is on the Crescent.
I think it's, like, 50-ish units more.
Does that feel fair?
I don't know, I just don't know.
We'd be billing for 50 units that never went live.
50 units that aren't going… so, the Arlo is live.
But their… their argument was that, oh, the Arlo shouldn't have been included, it's going into foreclosure, right?
It's change of management, technically, because the bank is taking it back.
Our contract with them has no out stipulations whatsoever.
So the Arlo's there, those units are there for the duration.
So I was just gonna say, okay, here's… let's say the crescent is 300 units, and the Arlo was 250.
I was just gonna take the difference and tack that on.
And the Arlo technically doesn't get removed.
The crescent doesn't technically come all the way on.
Does that make… right?
And we just bill for the difference of units.
I thought that might be a nice compromise for them.
So, the only thing, and the reason it's all this for you is just that it might change how much new MRR is, like, counted for you, I guess, just for this one property.
Okay.
But I feel like that's a fair enough compromise for them, at least until next year, and then we'll… Tack it on.
Yeah, okay.
Alright.
Anything else top of mind?
Any other customers, or any other issues that maybe I haven't caught up in yet or missed while I was out?
I don't think so, … Okay.
There was, like, a support ticket that came in from Cortera, from Ellen?
… Okay.
Ellen… what was her last name?
Ellen McFarlane?
Yes.
Colin McFarlane, here we go.
Historicals… Da-da-da…
Thank you, Brooke, blah blah blah.
Hi, Ellen.
Okay, she's figured it out.
I'm just gonna follow up with this, and just make sure she has everything she needs.
Okay, I think she did ask maybe one additional question that they didn't answer.
Okay.
Deleted it.
Are you able to help me get the historical unit level data?
Okay.
It sounds like she… It sounds like she did ask a question.
And then she figured it out, hopefully, but I'm just gonna reach back out and say, hey, everything good.
… All right.
How are you feeling about support, by the way, recently?
Because they had a big change, and we're mostly seeing that there's a small improvement, but there's not been, you know, it's short-term so far.
What's.
assessment right now.
Doesn't seem much better.
Okay, cool.
Good to know.
I personally don't feel very confident, and so I get really nervous, especially taking time out and putting them in my, in my, So, anyway, okay, good to know.
Just want to check your temperature, I guess.
And I tried to, like, put that in IQ Ops, so someone would answer me faster, and they were like… just send this to support, whatever.
I'm like, okay.
Great.
Yeah, okay.
It's frustrating, because, like, we know who can help, but they're all, like, process, process, process, and the process is still such a pain in the ass.
Yeah, I agree.
Okay.
Good to know.
We're also doing it, okay.
Awesome.
Anything else, Catherine?
I don't think so.
Do you have a good stretch?
I did.
I got a little sunburn, I got, I had a great time, so it was nice.
Good, good.
Thank you for asking.
We'll keep these every four weeks for now, but we can always change them if it's… if things don't come up as often.
Because I guess between you and I, it's really… now that Cortera's kind of Alfred.
Where it's kind of just gallery residential right now, right?
Yeah.
Alright, … Cool.
I will keep you in the loop on things, and we'll talk soon, alright?
Alright, sounds good.
Thanks, Phil.
See ya.
Bye.
Bye.
